title: mdtoc slugger candidate A: regex-based slugify
description: Owns projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_a.py. Implement slugify(text, seen) using a REGEX approach (re module). Conform to the vectors.py interface. Self-check with vectors.run_against and record passed/total in the task note. Stdlib only.
acceptance_criteria: Owns EXACTLY: projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_a.py. Touch no other file. Depends on T-023 (vectors.py) being done.
Implements slugify(text: str, seen: dict) -> str conforming to the interface documented in projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py.

APPROACH = REGEX-BASED: use the re module (compiled patterns) to lowercase, strip punctuation / keep unicode \w chars + hyphen, collapse spaces to hyphens, and dedupe via the seen dict (-1/-2 suffixes).
Self-check: import vectors and run vectors.run_against(slugify); record the exact passed/total (and any failures) in the task handoff note.
Method must be genuinely different from candidates B (unicodedata category) and C (github-slugger port) - this is a diversity-of-method tournament, not redundancy.
stdlib only; no import of the mdtoc package or the other candidates.

SHARED CONVENTIONS (all mdtoc tasks): Python 3.9+ stdlib ONLY, no pip deps. Package dir projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/ (namespace package; __init__.py is created only by T-028, so test files must add projects/mdtoc/ to sys.path and import as `from mdtoc.parser import ...`). Tests use unittest and must pass via `python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc`. No file listed here may be created/edited by any other task (disjoint ownership).
